title: UPSC Prelims — GS Paper I
subtitle: Civil Services General Studies Practice Set

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' is enshrined in the Indian Constitution through:
questions.explanation: DPSPs (Part IV) lay down the principles of social justice and welfare state that the government should aim for.

questions.question: Who among the following was NOT a member of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi was NOT a member of the Constituent Assembly. He was the moral force behind the freedom movement.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilisation is associated with which period?
questions.explanation: The mature phase of the Indus Valley (Harappan) Civilisation is dated approximately 2600–1900 BCE.

questions.question: Which ancient text deals with Indian medicine (Ayurveda)?
questions.explanation: Charaka Samhita (c. 1st century BCE–2nd century CE) is the foundational text of Ayurvedic medicine.

questions.question: The Eastern Ghats are discontinuous hill ranges that run parallel to the:
questions.explanation: The Eastern Ghats run roughly parallel to the eastern coast (Bay of Bengal) of India.

questions.question: El Niño is associated with which ocean?
questions.explanation: El Niño is a climate pattern of warm ocean surface temperatures in the central and eastern Pacific Ocean.

questions.question: Which of the following gases contributes most to the greenhouse effect on Earth?
questions.explanation: Water vapour is actually the most abundant and strongest greenhouse gas, though CO2 is more discussed due to human emissions.

questions.question: The 'Green Revolution' in India was associated with which crop?
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution (1960s–70s) primarily involved the introduction of high-yielding wheat varieties, championed by M.S. Swaminathan.

questions.question: Which Biosphere Reserve in India has UNESCO recognition?
questions.explanation: Both Nilgiri (2000) and Nanda Devi (2004) Biosphere Reserves have UNESCO World Network of Biosphere Reserves recognition.

questions.question: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) is primarily aimed at:
questions.explanation: PMJDY (launched 2014) aims at financial inclusion — providing affordable access to banking, insurance, and pension services.

questions.question: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was propounded in which case?
questions.explanation: The Basic Structure doctrine was established by the Supreme Court in Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala (1973).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Property was removed from Fundamental Rights by the 44th Amendment (1978) and made a constitutional right under Article 300A.

questions.question: The Sangam Age refers to the ancient history of which region?
questions.explanation: The Sangam Age (c. 300 BCE–300 CE) refers to the classical period of Tamil literature and culture in South India.

questions.question: The 'Swaraj Party' after the Non-Cooperation Movement was founded by:
options: Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Aurobindo Ghose
Lala Lajpat Rai and Bipin Chandra Pal
questions.explanation: The Swaraj Party was founded in 1922–23 by C.R. Das and Motilal Nehru to contest legislative councils.

questions.question: Which soil type is most suitable for growing cotton in India?
questions.explanation: Black cotton soil (Regur) is ideal for cotton cultivation due to its high moisture retention capacity.

questions.question: The Himalayan range is an example of which type of mountain?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas are fold mountains formed by the collision of the Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates.

questions.question: Which international convention deals with biodiversity?
questions.explanation: The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD), signed at Rio de Janeiro in 1992, addresses biodiversity conservation.

questions.question: Kaziranga National Park is famous for:
questions.explanation: Kaziranga (Assam) is a UNESCO World Heritage Site hosting about 2/3 of the world's one-horned rhinoceroses.

questions.question: The Preamble to the Indian Constitution describes India as a:
questions.explanation: The Preamble (as amended by 42nd Amendment, 1976) declares India a 'Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic'.

questions.question: The Bretton Woods Conference (1944) established which institutions?
questions.explanation: The Bretton Woods Conference (1944) established the IMF and the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank).

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution provides for the formation of new states?
questions.explanation: Article 3 empowers Parliament to form new states, alter boundaries, or change names of existing states.

questions.question: The 'Great Barrier Reef' is located off the coast of:
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef, the world's largest coral reef system, lies off the coast of Queensland, Australia.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Maratha Empire?
questions.explanation: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj (1630–1680) founded the Maratha Empire and was its first ruler.

questions.question: The Shimla Agreement (1972) was signed between India and:
questions.explanation: The Shimla Agreement was signed on 2 July 1972 between Indian PM Indira Gandhi and Pakistani PM Zulfikar Ali Bhutto after the 1971 war.

questions.question: Which committee recommended the three-tier Panchayati Raj system in India?
questions.explanation: The Balwantrai Mehta Committee (1957) recommended the three-tier Panchayati Raj system (village, block, district).

questions.question: The 'MGNREGS' (NREGA) guarantees how many days of employment per year to rural households?
questions.explanation: MGNREGS guarantees 100 days of unskilled manual work annually to rural adult members of households.

questions.question: Which of the following is a measure of income inequality?
questions.explanation: The Gini Coefficient measures income inequality within a nation — 0 represents perfect equality, 1 perfect inequality.

questions.question: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) relates to:
questions.explanation: PMFBY (launched 2016) provides financial support to farmers suffering crop loss due to natural calamities, pests, or diseases.

questions.question: The Non-Cooperation Movement (1920-22) was withdrawn due to:
questions.explanation: Gandhi called off the Non-Cooperation Movement in February 1922 after the Chauri Chaura incident, where a mob killed 22 policemen.

questions.question: Which river originates from the Amarkantak plateau?
questions.explanation: The Narmada and Son rivers both originate from the Amarkantak plateau in Madhya Pradesh.
